user_content: I've been thinking about all these graduations I've attended recently, including my niece's high school graduation ceremony last month on June 15th. It got me thinking about how proud I was of her and how fast time flies. Anyway, getting back to my own education, I'm curious about online courses that can help me prepare for data science certifications. Can you recommend some resources that can help me assess my current skills and identify areas for improvement?

here's another one of his emails. Give me three to five bulleted statements, about one or two lines of text each bullet, on what you learned about Justin through the following piece of writing: Build it and they will NOT come.
How many times have we heard this phase, “Build it and they will come!”
That's 100% total nonsense.
Honestly, this is the kind of mindset that made me wanna quit going to school.
I mean what they teach you in college, and lower education, especially in public schools, is total bull crap.
Basically, school teaches you how-to “be” and stay poor. Yes, that's right I'm knocking schools today.
Believe me, there's plenty of value in the education a student gets in public school systems, don't get me wrong.
And speaking of VALUE, it's funny how little people value the public library or public school system these days. But anyways...
A lot of the bologna they teach us in bid-ness school is to create a product and get that MBA.
Worse... invent something from scratch (which nowadays I strongly advise anyone reading to not EVER do ANYTHING from scratch, and will be something I will be talking more about.)
Why invent and innovate??? 
Why re-invent the wheel... over...and over... and over again like a damn hamster wheel?
The schools of business these days are pretty much saying that us “kids” best create something really “unique” and “what-people-want” garbage.
Then once the student makes this thing or whatever the deliverable they want to sell, it's time to:
START SELLING!
Yup, create a product and pick a market (or niche) to sell it to. Then, …[truncated]

I've been thinking of selling my old drum set that's been collecting dust in my garage for years. Can you give me some tips on how to take good photos of it for my Craigslist ad? I want to make it look appealing to potential buyers.

That's good to know. I'm thinking of getting a bike rack for my car too. I've been going to the nearby trail more often lately, and it's a pain to have to drive there with my bike sticking out of the trunk. Do you have any recommendations for bike racks that would be suitable for my car?

I think I'll prioritize dark and neutral colors for my tops and bottoms, but also pack a few lighter colors to add some visual interest. By the way, I've been trying to improve my packing habits and I've been paying attention to what I packed for my last few trips. Do you know how to help me create a packing list based on my previous trips?

I can definitely use my past trips to help me create a packing list. Speaking of past trips, I think I packed 7 shirts on my last trip to Chicago, but I only ended up wearing 3 of them. I'm thinking of packing lighter this time around. Do you think I should pack clothes that can be easily hand-washed and dried if needed?

I'm trying to plan a trip to Arizona and I was wondering if you could recommend some must-see attractions and activities. By the way, I've been to the Grand Canyon before with my family on a road trip across the American Southwest a few years ago, but I'm looking to explore more of the state on my own this time.

rewrite this very good piece and include some of the elements you mentioned for turning it into an origins story:

""I am Kadence, a 25-year-old musician from New York City who was raised in Jamaica. Music has been a constant in my life, a source of comfort and inspiration that has seen me through the ups and downs of my journey. From a young age, I was drawn to the rhythms of the Caribbean and the art of music-making, and it was in Jamaica where I first discovered my love for rap and hip-hop.

I started my journey as a rap artist under the name 'Kadence' in 2012, and it wasn't long before I became well-known in my home city of Mandeville for my skills as a producer, vocalist, and songwriter. I spent countless hours honing my craft, pouring my heart and soul into each beat, each verse, each chorus. I knew that music was my calling, and I was determined to make it my life's work.

So, in 2015, I made the bold decision to move to Toronto, Canada, to pursue my dream of becoming a successful musician. It was a difficult decision, leaving behind my family and friends, but I was driven by a deep passion for music and a fierce determination to succeed. And I am proud to say that today, I produce and record my own music, blending elements of rap and hip-hop, R&B, dancehall, and pop into a sound that is uniquely my own.

My music reflects my experiences, my emotions, and my journey. It is a testament to my resilience and my determination. But it is more than just entertainment, it is a source of comfort and inspiration for those who need it most. I use my songs t …[truncated]

1. The purpose of this section is to serve as an ever deepening framework for internal use behind the scenes with Kadence. It serves are a point of conclusion and reference for future content specifically for Kadence.

2. I want to include storytelling information in order to extract the parts of my life that would best serve the overall book we are writing. It is also a precursor to the coming sections which will further the story and will continue to develop over time as I learn more about myself and my audience.

3. I would like to present it as a story but written in an autobiographical style, as if it were the narration on top of a movie.

4. There are many, but I'm not sure how to define them right now. Some include my first experiences with love, sex, drugs, music, friends, betrayal, envy, and jealousy. Some other might include the things I've taken away from my life experiences and how I believe I might view the World differently from most people on the planet. I believe there is a way to clarify and grow my character in the eyes of my audience.

5. In this section, I believe the theme should just be more of an introduction to Kadence's reaching the point of transitioning from the ordinary world into the next phase of the hero's journey.

6. I want the reader to feel inspired, like they want to read more. Above all, I want them to feel touched, as if the whimsical nature of Kadence brings joy and intrigue to their heart and mind.

7. Not that I can think of... Maybe you can leave some room for me to fill in the blanks with my own stories and then …[truncated]
I'm planning a trip to Seattle and need help finding a hotel with a great view of the city. Do you have any recommendations?

I'm planning a trip to Las Vegas and I'm trying to pack light. Can you give me some tips on what essentials I should bring? By the way, I just got back from a trip to New York City and I realized I packed 7 tops and only wore 3 of them, so I'm trying to be more thoughtful about what I bring this time around.

I actually just got a new backpack-style carry-on that I love, and it's been a game-changer for staying organized on the go. Since I tend to overpack, I'm trying to be more thoughtful about what I bring this time around. Can you give me some tips on how to pack light for a 4-day trip to Las Vegas?

im going to keep feeding you more of Justin's writing. After, please write three to five bullets compelling statements about what you learned from Justin, through his writing: Justin McDonald is a copywriter who is passionate about the art of handwriting old ads. He rewrites hundreds of them daily, long-hand, for fun. His love for this practice is inspired by the belief that it helps him develop his copywriting skills and stay connected to the roots of the industry. Justin's daily handwritten ads can be found on his website and social media platforms, providing a unique perspective on the history of advertising and its relevance to modern-day marketing.

Justin's dedication to the craft of copywriting extends beyond his daily handwritten ads. He offers a monthly printed newsletter for $100 that covers a range of topics such as copywriting, small business management, entrepreneurship, personal selling, and personal development. He also provides daily email tips on these subjects, giving his subscribers insights into his copywriting process and approach to business. Additionally, he takes on freelance copywriting projects and consulting sessions remotely through various communication channels.

Justin's expertise in copywriting has also led him to create his own eBooks and publish a book on the subject. He frequently offers special deals and promotions for his products, ensuring that his subscribers have access to the latest and most relevant information in the industry. Furthermore, Justin engages in affiliate marketing and joint ventures, expanding his r …[truncated]
Can you ask me questions to help you write that section better and as deeply as possible?
I was thinking of bringing a backpack-style carry-on, do you think that's a good idea?
I'm thinking of packing 3-4 tops and 2-3 bottoms, like you suggested. But I'm not sure about the shoes, I tend to overpack them. How many pairs of shoes do you think I should bring?
